טפות.meaning: drop (of a liquid); a bit, tiny amount

להתעצבן.meaning: to become annoyed, to get angry, to become irritated

מוביל.meaning: to lead, to guide; to result in

מרחב.meaning: space (esp. a wide open space), room

פועל.meaning: to act, to operate, to do, to work

אפר.gloss: ash — buried under the floor from death camps

בראשית.gloss: in the beginning [also = name of book of Genesis]

הארץ.gloss: the earth / the land [modern, also = Israel]

ויהי.gloss: and it was [biblical past of "to be"]

שכם.gloss: Shechem [modern, also = a city in West Bank]

העקדה.gloss: the binding (technical term for the story)

יהודה.gloss: Judah (4th, of Lea — kingly tribe, source of "Jew")

יבק.gloss: Yabok river (wrestling site, in Jordan today)

דינו.gloss: Dayenu — "It would have been enough for us" (song)

יריחו.gloss: Jericho [modern, oldest city in the world]

רחב.gloss: Rahab (the prostitute who hid the spies)

ישעיהו.gloss: Isaiah (8th c. BCE, social justice + Messianic vision) [modern, name]

ירמיהו.gloss: Jeremiah (saw First Temple destroyed 586 BCE) [modern, name]

יחזקאל.gloss: Ezekiel (priest exiled to Babylon, dry bones vision) [modern, name]

יואל.gloss: Joel (locust plague, day of the Lord) [modern, name]

מיכה.gloss: Micah ("walk humbly with your God") [modern, name]

חבקוק.gloss: Habakkuk ("the righteous shall live by faith")

חגי.gloss: Haggai (return from exile, rebuild Temple) [modern, name]

מלאכי.gloss: Malachi (last of the prophets, "behold I send you Eliyahu")

צור.gloss: rock [modern, in "Tzur Yisrael" — Rock of Israel]

אבות.gloss: Avot / Pirkei Avot (Ethics of the Fathers)

חכמים.gloss: sages (the Talmudic rabbis as a group)

רבי.gloss: Rabbi (specifically: a Tanna with this title)

גזרה.gloss: rabbinic decree / fence around the law

טרפה.gloss: a torn or fatally injured animal

בשול.gloss: cooking (issue: cooked by non-Jew = bishul akum)

חגבים.gloss: most insects (some locusts permitted in some traditions)

הטבלה.gloss: tevila — immersion of new dishes in mikveh

עליה.gloss: aliyah (Torah honor, also = immigration to Israel) [modern]

סנדק.gloss: sandak (holds baby during brit, place of honor)

תשליך.gloss: Tashlich (casting bread into water, RH afternoon)

דרבנן.gloss: rabbinic (later than Torah, slightly more flexible)

בדיעבד.gloss: after the fact (still valid even if not ideal) [modern]

מרכז.gloss: center (Yesh Atid, Blue & White when existed)

האריאל.gloss: "the Ariel" (Religious Zionist party brand, name has changed)

הטכניון.gloss: Technion — Israel Institute of Technology, Haifa

ביט.gloss: Bit (P2P payment app, owned by Bank Hapoalim)

אריסון.gloss: Arison (Shari Arison — Bank Hapoalim controlling shareholder)

אוסם.gloss: Osem (pasta, instant soup, Bissli, Bamba — Nestle subsidiary)

במבה.gloss: Bamba (peanut snack, the iconic Israeli food)

אלביט.gloss: Elbit Systems (largest private defense, electronics + UAVs)

איסקר.gloss: Iscar (cutting tools, Wertheimer family — sold to Buffett)

קובנה.gloss: kubaneh (Yemeni Shabbat morning bread, baked overnight)

חילבה.gloss: hilbeh (fenugreek paste, the bitter green sauce)

לביבה.gloss: latke (Hebrew for the Hanukkah potato pancake)

זעצר.gloss: tzimmes (sweet stew of carrots, prunes, sweet potato)

גליל.gloss: Galilee (the region these dishes come from)

גזוז.gloss: gazoz (sparkling fruit syrup, retro Tel Aviv)

הקריה.gloss: The Kirya (IDF HQ in Tel Aviv)

שלדג.gloss: Shaldag (Air Force commandos / forward air controllers)

גולני.gloss: Golani Brigade (1st Brigade — symbol: olive tree)

בלגן.gloss: mess, chaos (THE most common word in Israel)

להחברל.gloss: to chat / shoot the breeze (from haver+Yiddish)

שלימיל.gloss: klutz (he MAKES things happen, bad ones)

מענטש.gloss: a decent, upright person — ultimate compliment

מידה.gloss: character trait (Hebrew, but central in mussar)

קליזמר.gloss: klezmer (Yiddish: kli + zemer = vessel of song)

שורק.gloss: Sorek (largest single-train SWRO plant in world)

יאני.gloss: I mean / kinda / so to speak

ידע.gloss: knew (Arabic ʿarafa, but root y-d-ʿ shared with Aramaic)

תפילה.gloss: prayer (Arabic ṣalāh — different root, same function)

אלוה.gloss: God (Arabic Allāh — same root ʾ-l-h, "the deity")

חג.gloss: festival (Arabic ḥajj = pilgrimage, same root ḥ-g/j)

יעני.gloss: I mean / that is to say

הכרמל.gloss: Carmel (Haifa upper district, Druze villages nearby)

כלכליסט.gloss: Calcalist — business daily, tech and economy

שירה.gloss: convoy (the supply convoys to the bloc)

נצול.gloss: rescue (of women and children before the fall)

מאורעות.gloss: events (the events of 1948, in memory language)

שכינה.gloss: divine presence — returning to the Land

צמצום.gloss: kabbalistic contraction — Lurianic concept Rav Kook reframes

אהבה.gloss: love — the central engine of his ethics

ציון.gloss: Zion — both place and metaphysical pole

שבות.gloss: return — to the Land, to Torah, to the self

שרון.gloss: Ariel Sharon (the leader who ordered it)

יהיה.gloss: he will be / there will be

לטיל.gloss: to hike / to travel on foot

מוקי.gloss: Mooki — early Israeli rapper, reggae-rap crossover

וסטי.gloss: Vesti — daily, founded 1992, largest Russian-language paper

להתפיס.gloss: to make peace with each other

הילולה.gloss: hilula (saint celebration, e.g. Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai)

הפלצל.gloss: le Pletzl (Marais, Paris IVe, historic Jewish quarter)

סטימצקי.gloss: Steimatzky — bookstore chain with French sections

ארצה.gloss: to the Land (idiomatic for arriving in Israel)

פאטיסרי.gloss: pastry shop (FR pâtisserie, niche term in olim circles)

מקרון.gloss: macaroon (FR macaron — confused often with the politician)

בקלוריאה.gloss: baccalauréat (HE name "bagrut" exists but olim use FR for the FR exam)

בונבון.gloss: candy (FR has fully replaced HE "sukariya" in many olim homes)

גאטו.gloss: cake (FR gâteau, especially for birthday cake — "ha-gato shel yom huledet")

פילו.gloss: filo / warka (thin pastry sheets for brik)

נורוז.gloss: Norouz (Persian New Year, kept in diaspora)

אנוסים.gloss: forced converts (Jews compelled to abandon Judaism publicly)

אסתר.gloss: Jewish queen, raised by her cousin Mordechai, hides her identity

התך.gloss: eunuch and messenger between Mordechai and Esther inside the palace

הגזרה.gloss: the decree — to destroy all Jews "young and old, women and children, in one day"

תחפושות.gloss: costumes — adults and children dress up; the theme is hidden identity

רעשנים.gloss: noisemakers — used to drown out Haman\

אקבטנה.gloss: Ecbatana — ancient name, Median capital, where Persian kings summered

דולמה.gloss: dolmeh — stuffed grape leaves with rice, meat, and herbs

תהדיג.gloss: tahdig — the prized golden-crispy bottom of the rice pot, fought over

אבגושט.gloss: abgoosht — lamb-chickpea-potato stew, eaten in two stages (broth then mash)

תורשי.gloss: torshi — pickled vegetables, sour-sharp, served with every meal

פאלודה.gloss: faloodeh — frozen rose-water dessert with thin starch noodles and lime

דוג.gloss: doogh — salty mint yogurt drink, served with grilled meats

שרבת.gloss: sharbat — fruit syrup drink, often rose, sour cherry, or quince

תפסיר.gloss: word-by-word Persian translation of biblical texts in Hebrew letters

דיואן.gloss: poetry collection — Judeo-Persian poets followed Hafez/Rumi forms in Hebrew script

מסנוי.gloss: rhymed-couplet form Judeo-Persian poets used for biblical epic

בוכארית.gloss: Judeo-Tajik of Bukhara/Samarkand — Tajik base + Hebrew/Aramaic, ~50K speakers Israel

משהדית.gloss: Mashhad dialect — Allahdad 1839 forced conversion, crypto-Jewish layer

לוטראי.gloss: cryptolect — Judeo-Persian secret vocabulary used for in-group communication

פרדס.gloss: orchard/paradise — from Old Persian paridaida (walled garden), entered Biblical Hebrew

אגרת.gloss: letter — from Old Persian "agarta", now standard modern Hebrew

אספרנא.gloss: diligently/promptly — from Old Persian, in Ezra

נשתון.gloss: royal letter — Old Persian official document

פיטנות.gloss: sacred liturgical poetry sung in service

דסטגאה.gloss: Persian modal system (~7 main modes, 5 derivatives)

גושה.gloss: melodic phrase / motif within a dastgah

תחריר.gloss: tahrir — vocal ornament with throat trill

קנון.gloss: kanun — plucked zither (shared with Arab music)

צדקיהו.gloss: Zedekiah (last king of Judah, blinded 586 BCE)

הנשארים.gloss: those who remained (in Babylon — the majority)

גאון.gloss: Gaon (head of Sura or Pumbedita academy, ~589-1038)

נשיא.gloss: Nasi (patriarch — title used in Eretz Yisrael)

מעברות.gloss: transit camps (plural — there were ~120 of them)

בורגול.gloss: bulgur — cracked wheat, the Mosul kubbeh shell

הספיגה.gloss: haspiga — the golden crust at the bottom of the tbit pot, the prized layer

סמבוסק.gloss: sambousek — half-moon pastry, cheese or chickpea or meat

לאפה.gloss: laffa — Iraqi tabun-baked flatbread, larger and thinner than pita

מלפוף.gloss: malfuf — stuffed cabbage rolls, Iraqi-style with sour pomegranate

במיה.gloss: bamia — okra stew with tomato and lamb, Iraqi staple

אינגריי.gloss: ingriyi — eggplant and lamb stew, slow-cooked, Friday lunch classic

מלוחייה.gloss: mluchiya — jute leaf stew, Iraqi-Egyptian-Levantine bridge dish

עמבה.gloss: amba — fermented mango pickle, Iraqi import that became Israel\

סילן.gloss: silan — date syrup, the Babylonian "honey" of biblical "land of milk and honey"

חלק.gloss: halek — thick date paste, used year-round and at Seder

תמרינדי.gloss: tamrind — tamarind paste, sour base for stuffed onions and dolma

שאומר.gloss: sheumar — sour pomegranate concentrate, used in malfuf and stews

זנגולה.gloss: zangula — fried saffron sweet, Iraqi version of jalebi/zalabia

קלתו.gloss: "I said" — the dialect marker (Muslim Baghdadi: gilit)

אמאלה.gloss: fronting of long /a:/ to /e:/ — bab → beb (door)

מיי.gloss: water — qeltu form; classical Arabic: mā\

שבאב.gloss: youth / guys — Arabic root, kept in family speech

גוי.gloss: non-Jew — Hebrew, used to mark community boundaries

חביבי.gloss: my dear — universal Arabic, kept in qeltu

תרגום.gloss: translation — many Iraqi Jews translated Arabic ↔ Hebrew

פסטאט.gloss: Fustat (Egypt, where Rambam wrote the letter)

המתנה.gloss: waiting (weeks to months for a flight)

פתח.gloss: short /a/ — distinct from kamatz, never merged

צירי.gloss: long /e:/ — kept apart from segol, no diphthong shift

מסורה.gloss: Masoretic transmission — Yemenite preserves both Babylonian + Tiberian features

מכלול.gloss: comprehensive grammar — Yemenites studied Radak alongside their tradition

טעמים.gloss: cantillation marks — Yemenite has its own unique melodic system

סחויק.gloss: sahawiq (alternate name for zhug, Yemeni Arabic)

חינה.gloss: henna — pre-wedding ceremony with henna paste

אלתור.gloss: improvisation — the singer adds verses on the fly

זחוג.gloss: Zhug — green or red hot chili paste

תושבים.gloss: Toshavim — the ancient indigenous Jewish residents

תפיללת.gloss: Tafilalt — ancient oasis Jewish region (southeast Morocco)

מראקש.gloss: Marrakesh — southern capital, large Toshavim community

מכנס.gloss: Meknes — imperial city, important rabbinic seat

קזבלאנקה.gloss: Casablanca — modern hub, post-19th c. growth

דמנאט.gloss: Demnate — High Atlas, deeply Berber-Jewish village

לעזיז.gloss: the dear one (al-aziz, Arabic with Hebrew article echo)

בסמיללאה.gloss: in the name of God (used by Jews too)

אזול.gloss: hello (Berber greeting, used by Atlas Jews)

נובה.gloss: nuba — a complete suite in one mode (11 surviving from 24)

מקאם.gloss: maqam — modal scale system shared with Arabic music

בולטות.gloss: boulettes — Tunisian-Jewish meatballs in tomato sauce

מרגז.gloss: merguez — spicy lamb-beef sausage, originally Jewish-Algerian

מטבוכה.gloss: matabuha — slow-cooked tomato-pepper salad, Algerian-Jewish staple

סכוג.gloss: skhug — Yemeni green chili paste (NOT harissa, often confused in Israel)

טביל.gloss: tabil — Tunisian spice mix (caraway, coriander, garlic, chili)

תלתה.gloss: three — interdentals collapsed to t/d (vs classical thalatha)

דהב.gloss: gold — interdental dh maintained in some Tunis variants, lost in Algiers

תלמסאן.gloss: Tlemcen — old Jewish center, archaic features preserved

גרנה.gloss: Grana — Livornese-origin Tunis Jews with Italian-flavored speech

חארה.gloss: hara — Jewish quarter (general Maghrebi term, used in both countries)

מזאב.gloss: Mzab valley (southern Algeria) — rare Berber-Jewish enclave

גרדאיה.gloss: Ghardaia — main Mzab town, mixed Ibadi-Berber-Jewish

אסגס.gloss: asgas — year (Berber); used in Mzabi Jewish calendars

תדאולה.gloss: oral tradition — most Mzab Jewish Berber survived only orally

בונזור.gloss: bonjour — French loan, ubiquitous in modern Judeo-Maghrebi

מרסי.gloss: merci — universal in post-Crémieux Algerian Jewish speech

מדאם.gloss: madame — Maghrebi Jewish address term, even in Hebrew today

בוטיגה.gloss: bottega (Italian) — shop, used by Tunis Grana speakers

גזטה.gloss: gazzetta (Italian) — newspaper, Livornese loan in Tunis

פיאצה.gloss: piazza (Italian) — square, in Grana speech of Tunis center

שעבי.gloss: chaabi — popular urban Algerian song idiom, Jewish-led golden age

דוכה.gloss: Doukha (Edmond Yafil disciple line) — Tunisian Mâlûf transmitter

ההילולה.gloss: Hilula — annual pilgrimage to El Ghriba, Lag BaOmer

פקילה.gloss: Pkaila — Tunisian Shabbat stew, fried spinach with white beans + beef

מהיה.gloss: Mahia — fig brandy, Tunisian-Algerian Jewish liquor

אספניול.gloss: Espanyol — what speakers themselves called it

חכיתיה.gloss: Haketia — northern Morocco variant (Tetouan, Tangier), more Arabic-laced

מורניקה.gloss: Morenika — "the dark-skinned girl," love song with many regional variants

רומנזה.gloss: Romanza — old narrative ballad, Iberian medieval form preserved

פסטל.gloss: pastel — small meat-filled pastry, Salonika origin

אלבונדיגס.gloss: albondigas — meatballs in tomato or lemon broth

יפראק.gloss: yaprak — stuffed grape leaves, rice and meat

קופטס.gloss: koftes — small spiced meatballs, often in soup

מינה.gloss: mina — matzah lasagna, Sephardi Pesach classic

קטניות.gloss: kitniyot — legumes/rice, permitted to Sephardim on Pesach

טישפישטי.gloss: tishpishti — semolina cake soaked in citrus syrup

מספן.gloss: masapan — Ladino marzipan with rose water

בורקסיה.gloss: bourekas-shop — found on every Israeli main street

רומנסה.gloss: romanza — medieval Spanish ballad preserved in Ladino

דיקלה.gloss: Dikla — singer, Egyptian Jewish heritage, Mizrahi-Sephardi sound

ריטה.gloss: Rita Yahan-Farouz — Iranian-born, sings Hebrew and Persian

דושנבה.gloss: Dushanbe — capital of Tajikistan, significant Bukharan community

נשירה.gloss: noshrim — those who chose USA over Israel

בכש.gloss: bakhsh (green herb plov for Shabbat, full of cilantro and dill)

רונדלי.gloss: rondeli (Mountain Jew cabbage rolls with rice and meat)

מנטי.gloss: manti (Central Asian steamed meat dumplings, larger than khinkali)

סמסה.gloss: samsa (baked meat or pumpkin pastry, Bukharan favorite)

תקמלי.gloss: tkemali (sour Georgian plum sauce, served with meats)

לואש.gloss: lavash (thin Caucasian flat bread, used to wrap meats)

נבאט.gloss: navat (rock candy crystals, served with green tea)

פיאלה.gloss: piala (small Bukharan tea bowl, no handle)

סופרה.gloss: supra (Georgian feast with toasts and song)

טאט.gloss: Tat / Juhuri — Mountain Jewish language, endangered

באקו.gloss: Baku — Azerbaijani capital, Mountain Jewish quarter

סוסנה.gloss: gold jewelry set worn by Bukharan bride

בגאלה.gloss: Bukharan ceremonial bread blessed at wedding

קיורולי.gloss: Kivruli (Judeo-Georgian) — Kartvelian language used by Gruzinim

בגרטיוני.gloss: Bagrationi dynasty — Georgian royal court, philosemitic to Jews

חינקאלי.gloss: khinkali — Georgian soup dumplings, eaten by hand

בייגל.gloss: bagel (boiled then baked, the NYC kind)

ביאלי.gloss: bialy (flat onion roll from Bialystok, no hole)

פסטרמי.gloss: pastrami (cured spiced beef, on rye with mustard)

חזרת.gloss: horseradish (red beet variety, with gefilte fish)

שלעפ.gloss: schlep — to drag oneself or an object tediously across distance ("I had to schlep to Brooklyn")

שמועס.gloss: schmooze — to network through warm informal chat (now corporate English)

קוועטש.gloss: kvetch — to complain habitually and unproductively

קיבעצן.gloss: kibitz — to comment unsolicited from the sidelines (poker, chess, life)

קלוץ.gloss: klutz — a clumsy person (literally: a wooden block)

גליטש.gloss: glitch — small malfunction; entered American English via Yiddish, now universal in tech

וויליאמסבורג.gloss: Williamsburg — Satmar stronghold, dense Yiddish street life

היימיש.gloss: haymish — homey, warm, of-the-community (a key Haredi cultural value)

פרום.gloss: frum — religiously observant (Yiddish, now Anglo-Jewish English)

פאלקסבינע.gloss: National Yiddish Theatre Folksbiene — founded NYC 1915, oldest continuous Yiddish theater

כאזארס.gloss: khazn / cantor — Yiddish musical theatre often had cantorial leads (Jolson, Rosenblatt)

פאלקסליד.gloss: folkslid — Yiddish folk song; "Tumbalalaika," "Bei Mir Bistu Sheyn," "Donna Donna"

דווקא.gloss: davka — precisely, on-purpose-against-expectation; untranslatable, increasingly used in Anglo-Jewish English

סטם.gloss: stam — "just because, no reason"; entering Hebish vocabulary

מייאמי.gloss: Miami metro (~530K Jews, retirees + young Israelis)

רקונסטרוקציוניזם.gloss: Reconstructionist (Mordecai Kaplan — civilization, not just religion)

לבנון.gloss: Lebanon (2009) — Cannes Golden Lion, war from inside a tank

הבועה.gloss: The Bubble (2006) — Fox, Tel Aviv life amid conflict

העובדה.gloss: Hatufim — Prisoners of War, basis for Homeland

בופור.gloss: Beaufort (Cedar, 2007 — last days at Beaufort fortress)

שכול.gloss: bereaved (a parent who lost a child)

החתון.gloss: Time of Favor / Ha-Hesder (2000) — settler ideology, Ophir best film

נורמן.gloss: Norman (2016) — Richard Gere as a NY macher, Cedar in English

סינמטק.gloss: cinematheque — Tel Aviv & Jerusalem temples of Israeli film culture

יוסי.gloss: Yossi (2012) — sequel a decade later, the survivor reckoning with grief

הקונגרס.gloss: The Congress (2013) — live-action + animation, Robin Wright as herself

תרילוגיה.gloss: trilogy — three films across a decade, same actress, same character

פוקסטרוט.gloss: Foxtrot (2017) — three-act structure on grief, Silver Lion + Ophir best film

הסודות.gloss: The Secrets (2007) — women׳s yeshiva in Safed, kabbalah & queer awakening

השוחט.gloss: The Slut (2011) — single mother in a moshav, debut feature, controversial

הסכסוך.gloss: The conflict — Israeli-Palestinian relations rarely centered as topic but always present as backdrop. Lemon Tree, The Bubble, Free Zone foreground it; even domestic dramas like Footnote reference it obliquely.

הומוסקסואליות.gloss: Homosexuality — Eytan Fox built a body of work; Graizer (Cakemaker), Shamriz (experimental) extended it. Israel׳s gay cinema is internationally recognized far beyond its size.

הפסטיבלים.gloss: The festivals — Cannes, Venice, Berlin recognize Israeli cinema disproportionately. Foxtrot (Silver Lion), Lebanon (Golden Lion), Footnote (Cannes screenplay), Waltz with Bashir (Oscar nominee). Domestic Sapir/Ophir prizes are the home circuit.

הבימה.gloss: Habima ("the stage") — first Hebrew theatre, founded Moscow 1917

ליאה.gloss: Leah — possessed bride, role created by Hanna Rovina

השפלה.gloss: humiliation — his protagonists are systematically humiliated

גורודיש.gloss: Gorodish (1992) — critique of Yom Kippur War general Shmuel Gonen

פולארד.gloss: Pollard — Lerner play on Jonathan Pollard spy case

אמסטרדם.gloss: Amsterdam — Yasur play, won Habima Prize 2018

ביאור.gloss: the Biur, Mendelssohn׳s German-into-Hebrew Torah commentary

המאסף.gloss: HaMeasef (The Gatherer), first Hebrew journal, Königsberg 1783

משכיל.gloss: maskil — a follower of the Haskalah movement

ברלין.gloss: Berlin — geographic cradle of the early Haskalah

סוסתי.gloss: The Mare (1873), allegorical novel on Jewish suffering

השילוח.gloss: HaShiloach (1896), flagship Hebrew journal he edited

אידיליה.gloss: Idyll — his signature genre (rural Jewish life)

המטפחת.gloss: The Kerchief — Yom Kippur Galician childhood

תירים.gloss: "Tourists" — short Jerusalem poem, taught in every Israeli school

המאהב.gloss: "The Lover" (1977) — first multi-narrator novel in Hebrew, post-Yom Kippur War

פימה.gloss: "Fima / The Third Condition" (1991) — Jerusalem dreamer, comic-tragic

דביר.gloss: Dvir (founded by Bialik 1922, now in conglomerate)

קישינב.gloss: Kishinev — site of 1903 pogrom Bialik wrote about

תימנים.gloss: Yemenite Jews — Schatz hired Yemenite silversmiths as instructors

יוגנדשטיל.gloss: Jugendstil — Central European Art Nouveau, a key input

שטיח.gloss: rug / carpet — major early Bezalel craft output

גלופה.gloss: lithograph plate — used for Bezalel poster prints

קיטש.gloss: kitsch — modern critical view of early-Bezalel decorative excess

באוהאוז.gloss: Bauhaus — Ardon brought the German modernist DNA to Bezalel

הרפורמה.gloss: the Reform — modernization of the curriculum, mid-20c

תחרותית.gloss: competitive — Bezalel admission rate is below 10%

תכשיטנות.gloss: jewelry — direct lineage to the Yemenite filigree tradition

שלכת.gloss: Fallen Leaves — 10,000 iron faces, Berlin Jewish Museum 2001

ארכיאולוגיה.gloss: archaeology — flagship discipline at the museum

יודאיקה.gloss: Judaica — ritual objects, costume, Jewish life cycle

קומראן.gloss: Qumran — caves where the scrolls were found 1947-56

בולות.gloss: bullae — clay seal impressions including biblical names

רודן.gloss: Auguste Rodin — Adam, Eve, Burghers of Calais on display

פיקסו.gloss: Pablo Picasso — Bathing sculpture in garden

האהלים.gloss: tents — recurring memorial / family vigil format

הערוצים.gloss: channels — Rubinger covered every major moment for Time-Life

נגטיב.gloss: negative — physical-film term central to his archive

גופענב.gloss: staged tableau — recurring Israeli photographic mode

קומרציאל.gloss: commercial — concern that some walls are now sponsored

תריסים.gloss: shutters — what Souza painted, visible only when shop is closed

גולדה.gloss: Golda Meir — Souza portrait, frequently photographed

נוע.gloss: Nova — recurring wall reference to Re\

מגילה.gloss: Megillah — scroll, esp. Esther for Purim

תימן.gloss: Yemen — origin of the filigree tradition

גרגש.gloss: gargush — Yemenite bridal headdress, embroidered with filigree

בזלת.gloss: Bezalel — academy, also descriptor for early-Bezalel-style craft

כתובה.gloss: ketubah — Jewish marriage contract, often illuminated

הקרדו.gloss: the Cardo — Old City Jerusalem, line of silver shops

מלכי.gloss: Meleke — soft cream-yellow Jerusalem limestone, used for the Kotel

תובזה.gloss: tovzah — rough-cut, hand-quarried look, common pre-1948

שבו.gloss: shabu — fine "rocked" finish, traditional masonry detail

סף.gloss: sill — window / door bottom in stone

הסינמתיק.gloss: Tel Aviv Cinematheque — concrete cube on Sprinzak

גו.gloss: rib / fin — common brutalist surface element

בפלש.gloss: plastering-over — cosmetic damage to original surfaces

ממילא.gloss: Mamilla — Old City Jerusalem promenade and mall, Safdie 2007

שנגהאי.gloss: Shanghai — Israeli architects active in China high-rise

מחיצה.gloss: partition — gender separation in Orthodox synagogues

אושפיזין.gloss: ushpizin — the seven biblical guests welcomed each Sukkot night

שדרה.gloss: boulevard — tree-lined median street, distinctive TLV form

החורבה.gloss: the Hurva — main Old City Ashkenazi synagogue, rebuilt 2010

נישה.gloss: niche — common to all four traditions

אנפאזה.gloss: Anaphaza (1993) — Echad Mi Yodea section is famous

פלדנקרייז.gloss: Feldenkrais method — somatic relative of Gaga

יתומה.gloss: orphan — Sara was raised in an orphanage in Jerusalem

שבזי.gloss: Rabbi Shalom Shabazi — 17c poet, principal Diwan source

קבוצית.gloss: Kibbutz Contemporary Dance Company — frequent guest

בולשוי.gloss: Bolshoi Ballet — many olim trained there

וגנובה.gloss: Vaganova method — defining Soviet classical technique

גלה.gloss: gala — typical Israeli ballet evening format

פואנט.gloss: pointe — in Hebrew often "al ha-kchavim" — "on points"

אימפלסטנץ.gloss: ImPulsTanz Vienna — major European partner festival

טלביה.gloss: Talbiya — Jerusalem neighbourhood housing the theatre

שירון.gloss: songbook — printed lyrics for the audience

הבארבי.gloss: the Barby — flagship Israeli rock club

התוכניה.gloss: the setlist / programme of the show

הורדוס.gloss: Herod — built the original Roman amphitheatre c. 22 BCE

העמק.gloss: The Valley — Jezreel, kibbutzim, agricultural heart

שהחינו.gloss: who has kept us alive (new things, holidays)
